aliases: Toasted Sourdough with Garlic Confit Butter

english: Toasted Sourdough with Garlic Confit Butter

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a sourdough bread entry (FOODON_00002683) but no specific entry for toasted garlic sourdough bread with garlic confit butter; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers bread and rolls as a category; no specific code for sourdough garlic toast exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559003 is the closest parent for bread as a substance; no specific sourdough garlic toast code exists.

serving.description: 2 slices (approximately 1 serving as served at restaurant)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of toasted garlic sourdough bread is approximately 100 g, equivalent to two medium slices of sourdough bread spread with garlic confit butter and toasted until golden.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, sourdough, toasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18027 adapted with garlic butter addition)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for sourdough bread (toasted), adjusted to account for garlic confit butter addition (~10 g butter per 100 g serving). Energy, fat, and saturated fat are modestly elevated relative to plain sourdough toast. Sodium reflects typical garlic butter preparation. Values represent a composite estimate for the as-served dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sourdough wheat bread
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 54 is based on sourdough wheat bread from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Sourdough fermentation lowers GI relative to standard white bread due to organic acid production. Toasting and garlic butter addition do not substantially alter GI. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (54 × 46.0) / 100 = 24.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g) from salted butter and bread
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice, as the sodium from garlic butter and sourdough bread can contribute meaningfully to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 54) and significant carbohydrate load (~46 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although sourdough fermentation lowers the GI relative to standard white bread, the carbohydrate content remains substantial; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict serving size to one slice and pair with protein or non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from garlic confit butter (~5.2 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The garlic confit butter contributes saturated fat that may raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions and consider requesting reduced butter or an olive-oil-based alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Toasted Garlic Sourdough Bread is a Western-style bread dish consisting of thick-cut sourdough bread that has been toasted until golden and crisp, then generously spread with homemade garlic confit butter. Garlic confit is made by slow-cooking whole garlic cloves in butter or oil at low temperature until they become soft, sweet, and mellow in flavour, losing the sharpness of raw garlic. The resulting butter is rich, aromatic, and deeply savoury. The sourdough base provides a characteristic tangy flavour from lactic acid fermentation, a chewy crumb, and a crisp crust when toasted. As served at Mingle – Funan in Singapore, this dish is positioned as a starter or side in an Italian-influenced casual dining context. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy food (~310 kcal per 100 g) with meaningful carbohydrate content (~46 g) and elevated fat from the butter (~9.5 g). The sourdough fermentation process lowers the glycemic index to approximately 54 (low GI), making it more favourable than standard white bread for blood glucose management, though portion control remains important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is moderately elevated (~480 mg per 100 g) due to salted butter and bread, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish contains gluten and dairy allergens and is not suitable for vegans or those with gluten or lactose intolerance.
common_user_queries: What is toasted garlic sourdough bread?
How many calories in garlic sourdough toast?
Is garlic bread suitable for diabetics?
Is sourdough bread healthier than white bread?
Can I eat garlic bread if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for toasted garlic sourdough bread with garlic confit butter exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sourdough bread data with a reasonable adjustment for garlic butter addition. GI is based on the closest analogous food (sourdough wheat bread) from the University of Sydney GI Database via the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base food (sourdough bread) is well-characterised in USDA data and the garlic butter adjustment is conservative and documented.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sourdough bread entry and is not reliably quantified for this preparation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for sourdough bread (toasted) with a proportional adjustment for garlic butter content; these are marked as adapted estimates. All -1 values reflect nutrients not individually reported in the primary source for this specific preparation. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 46.0) / 100 = 24.8 for a 100 g serving. This record was generated from a field survey data seed record for Mingle – Funan; the restaurant-specific preparation may vary. Allergens include gluten (sourdough bread) and dairy (butter). The dish is vegetarian but not vegan.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutritional profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sourdough bread data adapted for garlic confit butter addition.
Applied GI of 54 from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 for sourdough wheat bread as closest analogous food.
Added multilingual local names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 for this Singapore-served dish.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes field to satisfy G3.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent (Q8 consistency rule).
Set gi_category to 'low' per GI ≤ 55 threshold rule.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: closest_parent for all three ontology systems.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key type2_diabetes.
